name: Age of Enchantment
short_description: Tower defense deckbuilder where your towers are classes from D&D. Enchant towers with powerful buffs, sacrifice cards to forge new synergies, and select artifacts to customize your build.
detailed_description:
Please note: This game is not optimized for the Steam deck. Do not purchase if you plan on playing on a Steam deck. This will come as a future update!

Welcome to Age of Enchantment, a fusion of tower defense, deckbuilding, and D&D that you've always been waiting for.

Tower Defense + Deckbuilding + D&D

  • Artifact System:

    Unlock Artifacts by leveling up or finding them in game and purchasing them with Essence. Then, you can set these in your loadout to help you maximize your build and find the strongest synergies.

  • Class-Inspired Towers:

    Your towers take the form of classes from D&D. Start the game with the Fighter and Wizard, and unlock the Paladin, Cleric, Barbarian, and Warlock as you level up. Each tower has 3 different upgrade paths with 3 tiers level. 

    • Fighter

      • Duelist

      • Polearm Master

      • Great Weapon Master

    • Wizard

      • Lightning Mastery

      • Fire Mastery

      • Ice Mastery

    • Cleric

      • Domain of Light

      • Domain of War

      • Domain of Peace

    • Paladin

      • Templar

      • Shield Master

      • Inspiring Leader

    • Warlock

      • Hex of Weakness

      • Hex of Soul Leech

      • Hex of Withering

    • Barbarian

      • Zealot

      • Storm Herald

      • Berserker

Choose your loadout before each map to customize your strategy

  • Set your towers 

  • Choose your artifacts

  • Modify your difficulty with Skulls of Insanity - and be rewarded with massive bonuses.

Deckbuilding

Pray to the RNG Gods as you Sacrifice cards for the one that fits your build, or use the Sacrifice system to trim the cards you don't want from your deck.

Unlimited combinations

Fight across 10 different maps using your towers, enchants, spells, and artifacts in order to succeed. With over 50 artifacts, 100+ cards, and a combined total of 18 tier levels of towers, what synergies can you find?

reviews.review_text:
What a wonderful addition to the TD genre.

Is it ground breaking? No. It's not. The deck building is pretty minimal and, honestly, I never felt like I was building a deck. It's more cycling cards than construction. The challenge is self imposed. You have to unlock modifiers to ramp up difficulty for a better score. You have to want to do that. It's honestly a little easy at base difficulty.

The tower designs are neat and each feel worthy of use. You can go several different routes and still win. Didn't run into a "Well crap this is useless now I'm going to lose" moment. The relics are fun. Map designs are simple yet engaging.

Overall, I'd say get it. It could become something incredible, or continue to be a humble bit of unique fun. Totally worth a sliver of you lifetime.

Tl;dr The foundation is there but it really lacks the content.

I got this game after quite enjoying the demo and wanting for more. Yet sadly I was rather disappointed by the lack of this exact "more". 
In other words: the demo is pretty much the full game.
The full game really only adds some more towers. I liked these towers but really that's just too little content.
You can unlock 10 or so "new" maps as well, but the only difference on these is the shape of the path the enemies are taking. It's the exact same waves of the exact same enemies is the exact same order every single time. 
I guess there are a few more artifacts to unlock as well, but these aren't even remotely interesting enough to impact the gameplay in any meaningfull way. Neither do the terribly balanced challenge modifiers you can turn on.

I am really disappointed by this game. That's obviously not a good thing but wouldn't have been possible if there hadn't been something I liked in the first place. And there are quite a few things:
The foundations are there! the Card fusion and upgrade system could need some more work but the basic Idea is fun already. The towers need some balancing and a lot of bugfixing and maybe a rework here and there, but overall they were interesting and if they were a bit more fleshed out they would be really fun to mix and match.
The soundtrack is nice, the visuals are nice the enemy diversity is good for a first level. Or in Short: the foundation of this game is good. 

But looking further there are lots of issues. A few of the game mechanics are flawed by design. The Dot stats for example. Dot damage scaling with base damage and everything dying in 1 or 2 attacks anyway means dot damage is miniscule no matter how much you boost it. Armor is another stat that hardly matters, so are % of hp damage, and your life points. 

Balance between cards is horrendous. And the game could really use some more deck management abilities. Maybe a way to keep cards in your hand. I think it should be possible to use cards of a highter tier in fusions with cards of a lower tier (in a way rerolling them). 
What the game really needs the most though is more content. More enemy types, different waves on different maps, Boss waves, ... and MORE waves, it shouldn't be 25 waves every time, half your synergies won't even be online at that point. 

If you want to experience the game just play the demo, you won't be missing anything. If you then want to support the creator get the game anyway, but don't expect much more content.

Very fun dnd themed tower defense game. Decently difficult without feeling absurd or too easy. Can be repetitive, but it's a tower defense game, what do you expect? My only real complaint is wanting more classes and an endless mode (unless i just haven't unlocked it yet.) I bought it on the introductory sale, but i still think $10 is perfectly reasonable for the game!

I am fully convinced the music in the game is AI generated (which is a bit distracting to me), and I didn't see this game listed with an "AI Generated Content Disclosure". The gameplay seemed alright but I just wasn't connecting with it for some reason so I didn't continue.

Played the demo enough to make me buy it. I like how each tower type has its own set of specializations that it can upgrade into. The deckbuilding feels fun and the artifacts feel enjoyable to add into the game. I want more out of the game (maps/artifacts/challenges/cards/etc) but hard to complain about that when I already put 8 hours into a game I bought for $8.

Oh also, works fine on steam deck. Just need to set one of the buttons to be a shift key and it works. Maybe small-ish text but that is it.
